title: WatchCommandResponse
description: Response from the OpenClaw backend back to the Apple Watch, formatted for low-bandwidth and quick audio/text output.

reportMeta.description: Structured report metadata that mirrors the watch/phone handoff fields so clients can avoid parsing the freeform report text.

status.description: The processing status of the command.

transcript.description: The backend-transcribed text of the user's audio.

summary_text.description: A short, Watch-optimized summary (< 200 chars) of the action taken or the job's current state. Suitable for Watch screen.

tts_audio_data.description: Base64 encoded Text-to-Speech audio of the summary_text. Allows the Watch to play audio immediately.

tts_format.description: The format of the TTS audio data.

requires_phone_handoff.description: True if the backend determined the agent's output is too complex for the Watch (e.g., contains code, long lists, large diffs).

handoff_url.description: A deep-link URL (e.g., ceviz://job/123) for the companion iPhone app to open the full detailed view. Only required if requires_phone_handoff is true.

deep_link.description: Canonical deep-link URL for the companion iPhone app. Prefer this over handoff_url in new clients.

handoff_reason.description: Machine-friendly explanation for why the phone handoff is needed.

job_id.description: The OpenClaw job ID associated with this command, if a job was created or interacted with.

phone_report.description: A longer phone-oriented status/report string. May be a lightweight initial report while the async OpenClaw job is still running.

preview_sections.description: Thin structured preview sections for watch/iPhone continuation cards, aligned with report section semantics.

next_actions.description: Structured next-action affordances for phone continuation surfaces.
